title: UNION with JOIN USING puts results under wrong column
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE T3 ( Id int auto_increment primary key, T3_Val varchar(255) NOT NULL );
SELECT * FROM T1 LEFT JOIN T3 ON T3.Id = T1.Id UNION SELECT * FROM T2 RIGHT JOIN T3 ON T3.Id = T2.Id WHERE T3.Id IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM T1 LEFT JOIN T2 ON T2.Id = T1.Id UNION SELECT * FROM T1 RIGHT JOIN T3 ON T3.Id = T1.Id WHERE T1.Id IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE T4 ( Id int NOT NULL, T4_Val decimal(10,2) DEFAULT 0.00, PRIMARY KEY (Id) );
key_bug_features: JOIN USING causes incorrect column mapping in UNION results
